pattern: A binary search tree's ordering is itself information: a plain preorder walk of the values, with no null markers at all, carries enough evidence to rebuild the exact same shape by tightening a lower/upper bound as you go.
hints: The general binary-tree codec needs an explicit marker for every missing child; a BST gives you a second source of structure for free — where would you look for it?
Emit only the values in preorder; when rebuilding, feed each subtree an open interval of values it is allowed to contain instead of an explicit shape.

Consume the preorder values one at a time: a value only becomes the next node if it still falls inside the current (lower, upper) bound, otherwise that subtree is done — recurse with (lower, val) on the left and (val, upper) on the right.

edge_inputs.description: two-node tree with only a right child
mixed-shape tree with branching on both sides
left-skewed decreasing chain — no right children anywhere
right-skewed increasing chain — no left children anywhere
negative and six-digit magnitude values mixed in the same tree

stress.description: a random-size BST built by inserting distinct sampled values one at a time, then read back as level order
